sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Douglas County, "Lake Tahoe and tributaries" listing
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish. Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: CR 25-16 — Douglas County CLOSED WATERS (Edgewood Creek not listed as closed; only Glenbrook Creek is)
sources.quotedText: Topaz Lake within the jetties of Topaz Marina. Lake Tahoe within the boat launch area, inside the jetty at the Cave Rock boat ramp, north end. Glenbrook Creek from Lake Tahoe to Highway 50.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: USGS 10336765 — EDGEWOOD CK AT LAKE TAHOE NR STATELINE, NV (streamgage confirming Edgewood Creek is a Lake Tahoe tributary at Stateline, Douglas Co., NV)
sources.quotedText: EDGEWOOD CK AT LAKE TAHOE NR STATELINE, NV

sources.title: USGS Water-Resources Investigations Report 02-4030 — Environmental Setting (Stateline, NV area / Edgewood Creek watershed)
sources.quotedText: Numerous small ponds along Edgewood Creek, including one just above Edgewood Creek at Stateline, can affect quality of water and streamflow.

parameters.note: Edgewood Creek is a south-shore Lake Tahoe tributary lying entirely within Douglas County, Nevada (it flows west through the Edgewood Tahoe grounds into Lake Tahoe at Stateline; USGS streamgage 10336765 is named 'Edgewood Ck at Lake Tahoe nr Stateline, NV'). It is not named individually in CR 25-16, so the applicable Douglas County listing is 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' — open year around. That Lake-Tahoe-tributaries row (rather than the county's general 'All waters' row) governs Lake Tahoe's tributary streams, as confirmed by the parallel Washoe County treatment where specific tributary creeks (Third, Incline, Wood) are individually closed 'from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway.' Edgewood Creek is NOT in Douglas County's closed-waters list (only Glenbrook Creek is), so it is open all year.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round (Nevada / Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing; Edgewood Creek is a Lake Tahoe tributary and is not in the county's closed-waters list).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit: 5 game fish daily, 10 game fish in possession. 'Game fish' is the combined coldwater/warmwater game-fish count; no species sub-limit is listed for this row in CR 25-16.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: 5 game fish per day, 10 in possession (Nevada Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish.

parameters.note: The 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' row carries restricted hours (1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset) — narrower than Douglas County's general 'All waters' rule of any hour of the day or night.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing permitted from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers 12 and older. Edgewood Creek is a Nevada tributary (Douglas Co.), not the open water of Lake Tahoe; the Lake Tahoe CA/NV reciprocal-license arrangement is written for fishing anywhere ON the lake, so a Nevada license (not a California license alone) should be carried on the creek.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; none required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
